code:
def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    sma_period = params['sma_period']
    atr_period = params['atr_period']
    entry_mult = params['entry_multiplier']
    sma = ta.trend.sma_indicator(df['close'], window=sma_period)
    atr = ta.volatility.average_true_range(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=atr_period)
    distance = (df['close'] - sma) / atr
    signals = np.where(distance < -entry_mult, 1, np.where(distance > entry_mult, -1, 0)).astype(int)
    return pd.Series(signals, index=df.index, dtype='int')
rationale: Silver exhibits mean-reverting behavior due to industrial supply-demand shocks and speculative excess; when price deviates significantly from its moving average measured in ATR units, it tends to revert, providing a simple robust signal.
